category.label: Practices
category.description: How successful quant teams actually work: hypothesis-first research, data hygiene, decision logs, post-mortems. The boring parts that turn one-off backtests into a portfolio of repeatable, debuggable strategies.

entries.title: Quant Research Workflow
entries.subtitle: From idea to live trade: hypothesis, data prep, exploratory testing, formal validation, paper trade, live, monitor, retire. The lifecycle most successful teams follow.

external_links.title: López de Prado — Advances in Financial Machine Learning (research process)

entries.title: Data Hygiene (Point-in-Time, Splits, Survivorship)
entries.subtitle: Point-in-time databases, corporate action handling, survivorship bias, look-ahead from restatements, and why getting the data right is 70% of the job.

entries.title: Hypothesis-First Research
entries.subtitle: Why specifying your prior — and the falsification test — before you look at data prevents p-hacking from eating your whole research budget.

external_links.title: Munafò et al. — A manifesto for reproducible science

entries.title: Decision Journals and Strategy Logs
entries.subtitle: Why every research result, parameter choice, and live trade should be journaled — and what your log should record so future-you can debug today's PnL.

external_links.title: Kahneman et al. — Before You Make That Big Decision (HBR)
